_comment: Asked once per expected component on each page. The runner instantiates {component} with the component description from design.json. Stubbed in v1 — see grading/judge/runner.py.

questions.text: Is the visual presence of '{component}' on the agent's page consistent with the reference — visible if visible in the reference, absent if absent there?
Is the count of '{component}' close to the reference — within ±1 when the reference shows fewer than 6 instances, otherwise within ±20%?
Is '{component}' placed in the same region as in the reference?
Does '{component}' on the agent's page have the same visual class as on the reference (button vs. card vs. row, etc.)?
Does '{component}' on the agent's page show the same sub-elements as on the reference (e.g., for a card both must have image + title + body)?
Is '{component}' on the agent's page visually similar in size to the reference — neither noticeably smaller nor larger?
Does the styling treatment of '{component}' on the agent's page match the reference's — filled vs. outlined, light vs. dark surface, flat vs. shadowed, rounded vs. square corners?
